libraryObject.name: Splunk
libraryObject.description: Version 0.15 27.Mar.2026 by PP. For support requests use splunk@compek.net Changes: 27.Mar.2026 - URL Category always logged now, Syslog severity changed from 4 (warn) to 6 (info), URL shorted to max 16000 characters for properly trustedsource categorization. 22.Jun.2025 - fixed extraction of referer domain, added Authentication.Method.RawCredentials (to detect Basic-over-NTLM), configured NextHopProxy.Address 03.Aug.2024 - fixed ua2 in User-Agent and in Web Data Model

listEntry.description: Mac OS X disk image file
ActiveMime MSO object with VBA project
MS Office 2003 Excel Document in XML format
MS Visio Document in XML format
MS Office 2003 Word Document in XML format
Mac OS X disk image file
ActiveMime MSO object with VBA project
MS Office 2003 Excel Document in XML format
MS Visio Document in XML format
MS Office 2003 Word Document in XML format

list.name: SSL Cipher Suites Map
list.description: openssl ciphers -V (use most recent version, 1.1.1j with all enabled ciphers). Alternatively use https://testssl.sh/openssl-iana.mapping.html

configurationProperty.value: <list version="1.0.3.46" mwg-version="12.2.21-56040" classifier="Other" systemList="false" structuralList="false" defaultRights="2"> <description></description> <content> <listEntry> <entry>com.scur.category.130</entry> <description></description> </listEntry> </content> </list>

<list version="1.0.3.41" mwg-version="7.4.2-17140" classifier="Other" systemList="false" structuralList="false" defaultRights="2"> <content> <listEntry> <entry>com.scur.category.149</entry> <description></description> </listEntry> </content> </list>

configuration.name: Default Streaming Detection
configuration.description: Default configuration for the streaming detection.

configuration.name: Default
configuration.description: Default configuration for certificate chain filter.

configurationProperty.value: <list version="1.0.3.41" mwg-version="7-1352901515574" classifier="Other" systemList="false" structuralList="false" defaultRights="2"> <content> <listEntry> <entry>com.scur.category.105</entry> <description></description> </listEntry> <listEntry> <entry>com.scur.category.149</entry> <description></description> </listEntry> <listEntry> <entry>com.scur.category.176</entry> <description></description> </listEntry> </content> </list>

ruleGroup.name: Splunk
ruleGroup.description: Version 0.15 27.Mar.2026 by PP. For support requests use splunk@compek.net Changes: 27.Mar.2026 - URL Category always logged now, Syslog severity changed from 4 (warn) to 6 (info), URL shorted to max 16000 characters for properly trustedsource categorization. 22.Jun.2025 - fixed extraction of referer domain, added Authentication.Method.RawCredentials (to detect Basic-over-NTLM), configured NextHopProxy.Address 03.Aug.2024 - fixed ua2 in User-Agent and in Web Data Model

rule.name: DateTime.ToISOString (2010-03-22 11:45:12)
rule.description: A string representation of current date and time in ISO format (for example "2010-03-22 11:45:12")

rule.name: DateTime.ToISOString with Milliseconds (2010-03-22 11:45:12.123) Case1
rule.description: extracted from ISO8601 if milliseconds are present

rule.name: DateTime.ToISOString with Milliseconds (2010-03-22 11:45:12.123) Case2
rule.description: if no milliseconds in ISO8601 present: 2021-02-11T22:45:47Z, just add 000 to ISOString

rule.name: For following timestamp formats props.conf need to be adjusted!!
DateTime.ToGMTString (Mon, 22 March 2010 11:45:36 GMT)
rule.description: A string representation of current date and time in GMT (Greenwich Mean Time) format (for example, "Mon, 22 March 2010 11:45:36 GMT")

rule.name: DateTime.ToISO8601String (2016-01-26T11:45:36.695Z) - not recommended
rule.description: A string representation of current date and time in ISO8601 format (for example, "2016-01-26T11:45:36.695Z")

rule.name: DateTime.ToNumber (Unix epoch time - 1512915182)
rule.description: The number of seconds since 1.1.1970 (Unix epoch time)

rule.name: DateTime.ToWebReporterString ("29/Oct/2010:14:28:15 +0000")
rule.description: A string representation of current date and time in web reporter format (for example, "29/Oct/2010:14:28:15 +0000")

rule.name: DateTime.Date.ToString ("%hh:%mm:%ss")
rule.description: 14:53:12 - for realtime logs splunk will identify the data based on the current clock time

rule.name: Use Connection.IP as Client.IP (recommended for deployments without loadbalancer or downstream proxy)
rule.description: Stores the IP of the client

rule.name: Client.OriginalSourceIP (provided by MCP)
rule.description: Stores the IP of the client, provided by MCP or via IPSec

rule.name: Use Client.IP (only if there is another downstream LB/Proxy between Client and Proxy, that provides X-Forwarded-For)
rule.description: Stores the IP of the client. The proxy extracts a header value to build this property

rule.name: Anonymize Client IP (reversable with a password)
Pseudo anonymize Client IPv4 (modify last IPv4 octet)
Pseudo anonymize Client IPv4 (modify first three IPv4 octets)
Anonymize Client IPv4 (replace last octet with XXX)
User (as is), converted to lower case
Discard full name (separated by comma), use short string only
Action - error (based on the template name)
Action - blocked (403) + FailureDescription: Application response 403 (i.e. blocked in Error Handler)
Action - allowed for anything else. No need to set, as it is default value already
rule.description: CIM definition for Web.action is "The action taken by the server or proxy". 4xx (except 403 and 407) and 5xx are not action taken by the proxy, so they should be considered "allowed".

rule.name: Another option is to use Action.Names property that returns "Allow" or "Block"
sanitize URL (replace '=' with '%3D' to prevent KV extraction of URL parameters)
sanitize URL (replace '"' with '%22' to avoid parsing problems)
encode URL (replace unsupported characters in URL)
Log URL (not needed, see comment)
rule.description: Splunk can rebuild full URL using URL.Protocol+URL.Host+URL.Path+URL.ParametersString

rule.name: shorten URL.Path to the first not alphanum character
Fix for very long URL >16000 characters (TrustedSource URL categorization and Web Reputation are skipped for urls longer than 16000 bytes)
URL Category/Categories short - Try to get the cached value
Get URL Categories in Logging Cycle if the URL Filter was bypassed
Set URL Categories for internal Domains
Set as not categorized anything else
Replace comma (major breaker) with dot (minor breaker) to allow extraction with PREFIX

ruleGroup.name: Content-Type
ruleGroup.description: instead of writing it here, compare with ensured MT

rule.name: Skip if no User-Agent Header exists
Extract User-Agent String and save it to variable
Get User-Agent from CONNECT if it is missing in HTTP Tunnel
sanitize User-Agent (replace '=' with '%3D' to prevent KV extraction of URL parameters)
sanitize User-Agent (replace '"' with '%22' to avoid parsing problems)
Create short User-Agent for Internet Explorer (Windows)
Create short User-Agent for Internet Explorer 11 (Windows)
encode User-Agent (replace unsupported characters in URL)
User-Agent 2 for TERM/PREFIX acceleration (All characters except a-z, A-Z, 0-9, and minor breakers are replaced with an underscore). Also need to be enabled in Web Data Model > User Agent 2
response_time (LSLRS only)
rule.description: response_time can be just LSLRS if the sending time is not relevant

rule.name: Fix response_time if it is negative
Extract Referer_Domain using URL.Domain (saving the real URL to a variable and restore it after extraction)
DO NOT PUT ANY FIELDS BEFORE THIS LINE - FIXED FIELD FORMAT - can be removed in new format
rule.description: First fields have a fixed order, the parsing will break if it changes.

rule.name: Add host field (name of the proxy)
rule.description:
Set it explicitly instead of relying on host extraction from a syslog header or rDNS. Read a "host extraction" section in a README. This field must be placed in the first 4096 bytes to be extracted (see LOOKAHEAD in transforms.conf).
To enable: local/transforms.conf [rewrite_host_from_host_field] REGEX=\shost=(\S+) DEST_KEY= MetaData:Host FORMAT = host::$1 local/props.conf [mcafee:webgateway:custom] TRANSFORMS-rewrite_host_from_host_field = rewrite_host_from_host_field

rule.name: User-Agent 2 (without major breakers) for TERM/PREFIX acceleration. Also need to be enabled unter User-Agent > User-Agent 2
rule.description: Consider to change the criteria to "always" if you want to log empty user-agent values. In the MWG Splunk App navigate to User-Agents+ view.

rule.name: URL.ParametersString (seldom required, increases the event length, but can be helpful to understand why good domain (like google) has bad reputation.
Content Type (from Header)
rule.description: The content-type of the requested HTTP resource

rule.name: URL.Destination.IP (only if MWG can resolve it)
URL.Domain if it doesnt equal label+TLD
rule.description: don't assume that URL Domain contains just two last labels, consult publicsuffix: URL.Host -> URL.Domain bbc.co.uk -> bbc.co.uk safebrowsing.googleapis.com -> safebrowsing.googleapis.com

rule.name: if both ensured Media-Type and Content-Type are empty
rule.description: for example a pixel tracker with 204 response

rule.name: if ensured Media-Type equals already logged Content-Type then dont report it twice here
Log Content-Type from Header if it doesnt equal ensured Media-Type
Ensured Media Types (and replace application/x-empty -> empty)
Set threshold for header logging (Bytes of length)
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Set threshold for header logging (Bytes of length)
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Get long Header each by one
Erase header from list after reading
Headers-View: Get All Request Headers and convert to a list
rule.description: some characters need to be encoded to avoid errors in ReplaceFirstMatch/ReplaceAllMatches steps. A potential MWG bug: a string a=1\\r\nb=2 will not be converted to a string list with String.ToStringList. The first two backslashes considered to be one backslash. PP10.Oct.2022

rule.name: Headers-View: remove User-Agent request header from list
Headers-View: remove Proxy-Authorization request header from list
Headers-View: remove Cookie request header from list
Headers-View: remove Referer request header from list
Headers-View: Log All Request Headers using hrq_ prefix
rule.description: some characters need to be encoded to avoid errors in ReplaceFirstMatch/ReplaceAllMatches steps. A potential MWG bug: a string a=1\\r\nb=2 will not be converted to a string list with String.ToStringList. The first two backslashes considered to be one backslash. PP10.Oct.2022

rule.name: SSL.Client.Cipher.KeyExchangeBits (TODO check with NULL encryption)
rule.description: Normalized strength (symmetric) of the weakest link during the key exchange

rule.name: SSL.Server.Cipher.KeyExchangeBits
rule.description: Normalized strength (symmetric) of the weakest link during the key exchange

rule.name: SSL.Client.Cipher is not empty and exists in Map
SSL.Client.Cipher is not empty and doesnt exists in Map
SSL.Server.Cipher is not empty and exists in Map
SSL.Server.Cipher is not empty and doesnt exists in Map
Connection.SSL.TransparentCNHandling
rule.description: Will be true for ssl connections where the CN is not known until the server handshake is done

rule.name: SSL.Server.CertificateChain.Issuer.CNs
rule.description: The issuer common names of the certificate chain (bottom-up including the self-signed root CA, empty without certificate verification)

rule.name: SSL.Server.CertificateChain.Length
rule.description: The length of the certificate chain including the server certificate (0 without certificate verification)

rule.name: SSL.Server.Certificate.CN
rule.description: The common name of the server certificate

rule.name: DNS Lookup of dest_host if there is more than one IP
Log first XX Bytes of unrecognizable response payload
Authentication.Method extracted from Authentication.RawCredentials (to detect Basic-over-NTLM)
Authentication.UserGroups (filtered with Authentication UserGroups to log )
Authentication.FailureReason.ID
rule.description:
30.Jun.2022 - removed quotes
https://community.mcafee.com/t5/Web-Gateway/Troubleshooting-Authentication-Errors-in-MWG-dashboard/m-p/424355/highlight/true#M12541 Each failure ID has a failure reason string (human readable), the property is Authentication.FailureReason.Message. 0 No Failure - Authentication was fine 1 Unexpected Credentials - Authentication was out of expected order (applies to NTLM) 2 Unknown User - User doesnt exist in directory. 3 Wrong Password - Bad password (can apply in other situations, see https://community.mcafee.com/message/268185#268185) 4 No Credentials - No credentials were sent (ignore if authentication hasnt failed -- NTLM) 5 No Server Available - Directory server MWG attempted to contact was not reachable. 6 Proxy Timeout - MWG was communicating with a resource and it took too long to get an answer. 7 Server Timeout - ? 8 Communication Error - Server that MWG was communicating with shut down the connection. 10000 Internal Error -catch all

rule.name: Cache.IsCacheable
rule.description: True, if the response is cacheable and web cache is enabled

rule.name: Cache.Status
rule.description: TCP_HIT for a web cache hit, TCP_MISS_RELOAD for a miss, TCP_MISS_VERIFY if the data in the cache was outdated, TCP_MISS_BYPASS for bypass based on I/O load

rule.name: Cache.IsFresh
rule.description: True, if the response is validated or not read from web cache

rule.name: Rules.CurrentRule.Name (short if exists in Rule Map)
Rules.CurrentRule.Name (if doesnt exist in Rule Map)
Number of FiredRules (based on loghandler position)
Number of EvaluatedRules (based on loghandler position)
Client.NumberOfConnections
rule.description: Number of concurrent connections from this client to the proxy

rule.name: NumberOfClientConnections (restrict this rule to monitoring requests only)
Client.ProcessName
rule.description: Stores the process name that initiated the connection, e.g. provided by MCP

rule.name: Hostname of Client.IP (does a reverse DNS lookup!)
Log Client.IP from X-Forwarded-For if it doesn't equal Connection.IP
rule.description: do not enable if a load balancer or a downstream proxy is used

rule.name: Connection.Protocol
rule.description: The protocol that the client uses to communicate with the proxy (HTTP, HTTPS, FTP, IFP, SSL, ICAP, XMPP, TCP or SOCKS)

rule.name: Connection.Port (src_port)
rule.description: Stores the port of the client

rule.name: Connection.RunTime
rule.description: Connection run time (current time minus start time) in seconds

rule.name: Command.Categories
rule.description: Stores a list of categories that apply to the given command

rule.name: Protocol.FailureDescription
rule.description: Holds an error message when a protocol error has occurred

rule.name: Protocol.FailureDescription
rule.description: Holds an error message when a protocol error has occurred

rule.name: URL.Geolocation (local URL DB has to be disabled)
Log URL Length if more than 5000 charachters
Timer First Received First Sent Client
Timer Last Received Last Sent Client
Timer First Sent First Received Server
Timer Last Sent First Received Server
Timer Last Sent Last Received Server
Old format: Verify Log Structure and report if it is not correct (for a timestamp with a timezone: 2022-06-19 12:48:39 +0200)
New format: Verify Log Structure and report if it is not correct (for a timestamp with a timezone: 2022-06-19 12:48:39 +0200)
Verify Log Structure and report if it is not correct (for a timestamp without a timezone: 2022-06-19 12:48:39)
Check Log Line for potencially dangerous characters
Remove all newline characters (CR or LF)
Add a space at the end because many regexes based on its presence (not needed anymore starting from App version 5.0.0)
Send via Syslog
rule.description: 30.Jun.2022 - disabled sending syslog by default (disabled again, because the rule was enabled by mistake in 08.Jun.2022 version)

ruleGroup.name: RuleSet Library (do not enable it here)

rule.name: Skip all Rulesets if the top Ruleset is enabled
Get Application Info (place this rule anywhere after HTTPS Scanner but before any Block Rules, for example in URL Filter)
Get first 100 Bytes of unrecognizable payload
Track Malware (place this rule just before Block if Virus Was Found - Rule and change AntiMalware Settings)

stringValue.value: Sec-CH-UA, Sec-CH-UA-Arch, Sec-CH-UA-Bitness, Sec-CH-UA-Full-Version-List, Sec-CH-UA-Full-Version, Sec-CH-UA-Mobile, Sec-CH-UA-Model, Sec-CH-UA-Platform, Sec-CH-UA-Platform-Version
